design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Flagship near-miss with hand-derived nonroutine object: LP versus SVAR impulse-response identification under sign restrictions and non-Gaussian cumulant labels, repairing the banked eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade failure. Pre-anchor check: closest published theorem is Plagborg-Moller and Wolf 2021 Econometrica, which proves LP and VAR estimate the same population impulse responses under the same identifying restrictions; adjacent anchors are Read 2022 sign/zero algorithms, Wolf 2022 shock masquerading, Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne 2017 and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen 2017 non-Gaussian SVAR labeling, and Drautzburg-Wright 2023 independence-refined sign sets. Closest banked proposal is eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade, whose reviewer repeatedly reached publishability_tier flagship but rejected because c_w was not a canonical functional, sign exposure was normalized into tautology, and the PMW sanity reduction compared unequal LP/SVAR restriction sets. Our theorem is not that because this run must start from the repaired hand-derived object: define c_w(P) as the infimum of the support-loss to distance-product ratio over a compact local comparison set; define sign exposure as a non-normalized slack-distance channel; define common-restriction LP and SVAR sets with the same cumulant restriction before taking the PMW reduction; then state an iff channel frontier using independently computable determinant or support-function quantities, not tau defined as the gap itself. If the proposal becomes generic support-function geometry, a restatement that adding restrictions shrinks sets, or a tautological three-channel stack, pivot or stop early. Require concrete artifacts: a two-shock trigonometric witness with all three channel values computed, the parent chi_w collapse shown algebraically, and a sanity clause recovering PMW only after identical restrictions are imposed on LP and SVAR.

proposed_from.topic: Flagship near-miss with hand-derived nonroutine object: LP versus SVAR impulse-response identification under sign restrictions and non-Gaussian cumulant labels, repairing the banked eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade failure. Pre-anchor check: closest published theorem is Plagborg-Moller and Wolf 2021 Econometrica, which proves LP and VAR estimate the same population impulse responses under the same identifying restrictions; adjacent anchors are Read 2022 sign/zero algorithms, Wolf 2022 shock masquerading, Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne 2017 and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen 2017 non-Gaussian SVAR labeling, and Drautzburg-Wright 2023 independence-refined sign sets. Closest banked proposal is eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_upgrade, whose reviewer repeatedly reached publishability_tier flagship but rejected because c_w was not a canonical functional, sign exposure was normalized into tautology, and the PMW sanity reduction compared unequal LP/SVAR restriction sets. Our theorem is not that because this run must start from the repaired hand-derived object: define c_w(P) as the infimum of the support-loss to distance-product ratio over a compact local comparison set; define sign exposure as a non-normalized slack-distance channel; define common-restriction LP and SVAR sets with the same cumulant restriction before taking the PMW reduction; then state an iff channel frontier using independently computable determinant or support-function quantities, not tau defined as the gap itself. If the proposal becomes generic support-function geometry, a restatement that adding restrictions shrinks sets, or a tautological three-channel stack, pivot or stop early. Require concrete artifacts: a two-shock trigonometric witness with all three channel values computed, the parent chi_w collapse shown algebraically, and a sanity clause recovering PMW only after identical restrictions are imposed on LP and SVAR.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue/banked proposal is eid_lp_svar_nonequiv, whose v1 assumed chi_w(P) rather than deriving it, whose upgrade normalized sign exposure into a tautology, and whose upgrade2 left O(2) residuals and dominance checks promissory. This v1 differs by defining c_w(P) as a compact extremal support-loss-to-distance-product functional, using active-row sign exposure, imposing common cumulant restrictions before the PMW reduction, and adding Exhibit 9.1.","published_axis":"The closest published anchor is PlagborgMollerWolf2021, which supplies common-restriction LP/VAR equivalence. The gap is not LP-versus-VAR nonequivalence; it is the canonical channel constant and two-shock certificate for when an LP horizonwise relaxation fails to share one cumulant-labeled SVAR rotation. Wolf2022, Read2022UnitEffect, Read2022SignZero, GourierouxMonfortRenne2017, LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017, and DrautzburgWright2023 supply pieces of the geometry but not c_w(P) or the O(2) certificate."}
seed_list: Characterize an iff support-function equality frontier for common-restriction LP and SVAR sets under identical sign, zero, and cumulant-label restrictions.
Define a non-normalized sign-exposure slack channel that separates shock masquerading from unit-effect normalization artifacts.
Derive a determinant/rank cumulant-label condition under which a non-Gaussian component label selects the same shock as sign restrictions.
Give a weak-cumulant sharp identified-response envelope for common LP/SVAR restriction sets near Gaussianity.
Define c_w(P) as a compact extremal support-loss-to-distance-product constant and prove positivity/finiteness conditions.
Formulate the horizonwise LP pasting defect as the failure of separately optimized LP rows to share one cumulant-diagonalizing rotation.
Construct a two-shock O(2) trigonometric certificate with rotation/reflection residuals and dominance inequalities.
Give an algorithmic support-function equality test for sign/zero/cumulant LP-SVAR sets with finite candidate rotations.
Prove a parent chi_w(P) collapse when sign and pasting channels vanish, recovering the earlier cumulant-margin corner algebraically.
Characterize when independence-refined sign sets are nonconvex yet have the same support as the common cumulant-labeled SVAR set.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize an iff support-function equality frontier for common-restriction LP and SVAR sets under identical sign, zero, and cumulant-label restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Plagborg-Moller and Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LP and VAR estimators have a common population impulse-response estimand when restrictions are the same.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: LPs and VARs estimate the same impulse responses

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Equivalence frontier; nontrivial because restrictions are first equalized and the frontier uses channel distances, not set-inclusion bookkeeping.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Plagborg-Moller-Wolf because common restrictions already imply LP/VAR equality.
seed_details.rebuttal: PMW gives the equality corner; the seed characterizes when horizonwise LP relaxation separates after the same cumulant label is imposed.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not PMW because it studies the boundary between common-rotation equality and horizonwise LP support separation under cumulant labels.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the output is a new channel frontier with c_w(P), not a generic support-function shrinkage statement.

adversarial_referee.instance: The frontier could look like support-function bookkeeping.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The canonical c_w(P) and three independently computed channels are the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity might be asserted while only sufficiency is sketched.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal labels the iff frontier as Conjecture and gives a necessity route via pasting/transversality lemmas.

adversarial_referee.instance: The comparator could accidentally compare unequal restriction sets again.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The locked parameters and Theorem 1 impose identical sign, zero, and cumulant restrictions before PMW reduction.

seed_details.one_line: Define a non-normalized sign-exposure slack channel that separates shock masquerading from unit-effect normalization artifacts.

anchor_paper.author: Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: Sign restrictions are vulnerable to shock masquerading.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Normalization warning; nontrivial because the channel is slack-distance over active sign rows rather than E=1 scaling.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Read's unit-effect normalization problem renamed.
seed_details.rebuttal: Read motivates the failure; the seed defines a normalization-free sign-channel object for LP/SVAR set comparison.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Read's unit-effect paper because it avoids unit-effect normalization and targets sign-face slack in common LP/SVAR restrictions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: produces a new sign-exposure scalar with empty-row conventions.

adversarial_referee.instance: Read targets unit effects, not LP/SVAR support equality.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft uses Read only as normalization warning, not as the direct comparator.

adversarial_referee.instance: Slack could vanish by chosen scaling.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The slack is defined on response rows before unit-effect normalization.

adversarial_referee.instance: Other sign-restricted estimands may use the same channel.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The chosen target is the support function family indexed by w.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a determinant/rank cumulant-label condition under which a non-Gaussian component label selects the same shock as sign restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen
anchor_paper.claim: Non-Gaussian SVARs identify impulse responses subject to labeling ambiguities.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Necessary-and-sufficient label bridge; stronger than applying ICA because it isolates economic sign-label agreement.
seed_details.referee_objection: ICA already identifies shocks up to sign and permutation, so the label issue is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets the exact condition for agreement with sign-labeled economic shocks, which ICA papers leave outside statistical recovery.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen because it characterizes economic label agreement rather than statistical component recovery.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: determinant/rank condition is an independently evaluable focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could prove only that one determinant condition is sufficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed is framed as necessary and sufficient, with counterexample boundary required.

adversarial_referee.instance: Later weak-ID papers may subsume local-to-Gaussian cases.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed is population label agreement, not inference coverage.

adversarial_referee.instance: No support-function frontier unless tied to LP/SVAR sets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is used as the cumulant channel inside the support-function frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Give a weak-cumulant sharp identified-response envelope for common LP/SVAR restriction sets near Gaussianity.

anchor_paper.author: Hoesch, Lee, and Mesters
anchor_paper.claim: Standard non-Gaussian SVAR inference can fail near Gaussianity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-ID envelope; stronger if the envelope is sharp rather than qualitative weak-ID warning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an inference paper restated as population partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for the population envelope under common LP/SVAR restrictions, which is distinct from coverage repair.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Hoesch-Lee-Mesters because it constructs the identified-response envelope rather than a confidence procedure.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: sharp envelope is an evaluable support-function object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Envelope could be an unspecified infimum.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs support-function formula or finite-dimensional reduction.

adversarial_referee.instance: Weak higher-moment literature may already give robust sets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Known robust sets are inference objects, not common LP/SVAR population envelopes.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could cover multiple horizons and weights.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State the family indexed by w and H.

seed_details.one_line: Define c_w(P) as a compact extremal support-loss-to-distance-product constant and prove positivity/finiteness conditions.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: The prior upgrade needed c_w(P) as a canonical extremal value.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Define c_w(P) as a canonical extremal value

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical scalar repair; nontrivial because it replaces assumed curvature and avoids defining the threshold as the gap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is internal reviewer cleanup, not publishable economics.
seed_details.rebuttal: The scalar is the missing object that turns LP/SVAR cumulant-label disagreement into an evaluable frontier.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the banked upgrade because c_w(P) is an extremal quotient over a compact set, not an assumed multiplier.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: focal object is computed from support loss and channel distances.

adversarial_referee.instance: c_w could remain assumed rather than exhibited.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal includes Exhibit 9.1 computing it on a two-shock instance.

adversarial_referee.instance: Positive c_w may only be assumed.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The v1 scope admits global positivity is open and routes it through transversality.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims critical frontier but no critical delta.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The frontier scalar is support-loss-to-distance, not a sensitivity delta.

seed_details.one_line: Formulate the horizonwise LP pasting defect as the failure of separately optimized LP rows to share one cumulant-diagonalizing rotation.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: The pasting channel must compare horizonwise LP rows against a single common SVAR rotation.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: per-horizon optimizers against a single common SVAR rotation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Pasting defect; stronger than routine extension because it prevents the LP set from already ranging over one common rotation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just saying common rotations are common.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed isolates the horizonwise relaxation and gives a distance-to-pastability channel.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the failed upgrade because Q_LP is horizonwise before testing common cumulant compatibility.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the channel is a measurable compatibility defect, not a definition of the SVAR set.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be a renamed feasibility constraint.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It appears as one factor in an inequality for support loss.

adversarial_referee.instance: One horizon witness would be trivial.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The exhibit uses H=1 with two distinct horizon rows.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only failure implies separation may be shown.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture requires both vanishing-channel equality and positive-channel separation.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a two-shock O(2) trigonometric certificate with rotation/reflection residuals and dominance inequalities.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: The witness must spell out rotation and reflection quartic residuals separately.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: spelling out the rotation and reflection component quartic residuals separately

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Concrete witness; stronger if promoted beyond one hand-tuned law, weaker as standalone.
seed_details.referee_objection: A witness alone is not a flagship theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a support exhibit for the frontier it satisfies the concrete-object floor and audits nondegeneracy.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the banked witness because it computes channel values and reflected-component dominance.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS as an exhibit; standalone would downgrade to field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: A two-angle construction could be hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use it only as Exhibit 9.1, not as the headline theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: No generic-class promotion.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The generic claim is Conjecture 1; the witness verifies focal object computation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Reviewer wanted dominance over all O(2) candidates.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The exhibit lists rotation and reflection candidates and dominance checks.

seed_details.one_line: Give an algorithmic support-function equality test for sign/zero/cumulant LP-SVAR sets with finite candidate rotations.

anchor_paper.author: Read
anchor_paper.claim: Sign and zero restrictions can be encoded for nonemptiness and sampling algorithms.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability; stronger only if complexity and finite candidates are explicit.
seed_details.referee_objection: Read already gives algorithms for sign/zero restrictions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Read does not test LP/SVAR support equality under an additional cumulant-label channel.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Read because the candidate enumeration includes cumulant determinant and horizonwise pasting compatibility.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the algorithm returns equality/separation certificate, not just samples rotations.

adversarial_referee.instance: No theorem if only an algorithm is named.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need correctness and finite-complexity theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: Existing algorithms may subsume O(2) enumeration.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Cumulant-label/pasting equality is not a standard sign-zero feasibility test.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims equivalence frontier but offers a solver.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use as computational corollary, not chosen v1 headline.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a parent chi_w(P) collapse when sign and pasting channels vanish, recovering the earlier cumulant-margin corner algebraically.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: The parent chi_w(P) reduction must be algebraic.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the parent chi_w(P) reduction is algebraic

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sanity reduction; useful as theorem but not enough for flagship alone.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a corner-case check.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is included as Theorem 2 sanity support, not the flagship conjecture.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the banked upgrade because the collapse is explicitly derived after channel definitions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier as standalone

seed_details.one_line: Characterize when independence-refined sign sets are nonconvex yet have the same support as the common cumulant-labeled SVAR set.

anchor_paper.author: Drautzburg and Wright
anchor_paper.claim: Independence-refined sign sets can be nonconvex and disjoint.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Set-geometry bridge; nontrivial if support equality holds despite nonconvex topology.
seed_details.referee_objection: Support functions ignore nonconvex details, so the result may be convex-hull tautology.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed must state equality against the cumulant-labeled SVAR set, not merely convex-hull equality.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Drautzburg-Wright because it characterizes support equivalence to a common LP/SVAR cumulant set.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only if the condition is determinant/support based, not generic convexification.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could reduce to support of convex hull.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs independent determinant/support condition.

adversarial_referee.instance: DW is inference-focused, not population support equality.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use DW for nonconvex independence geometry, not as direct equality theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only prove one sufficient case.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need iff or downgrade.

proposed_from.literature_map: 1. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) anchor the sanity reduction: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses under common identifying restrictions. 2. Arias-Rubio-Ramirez-Waggoner (2018, Econometrica), Read (2022, Econometrics Journal), and Gafarov-Meier-Montiel Olea (2018, JoE) make sign/zero SVAR support-function geometry operational. 3. Wolf (2022, AEA P&P) names shock masquerading; Read (2022 RBA) shows unit-effect normalization can make sign-restricted sets unbounded. 4. Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne (2017, JoE) and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen (2017, JoE) identify non-Gaussian SVAR shocks up to label ambiguities. 5. Lanne-Luoto (2021, JBES), Montiel Olea-Plagborg-Moller-Qian (2022, AEA P&P), Hoesch-Lee-Mesters (2024, QE), and Drautzburg-Wright (2023, JoE) show that higher-moment and independence refinements are informative but fragile, weak, or nonconvex. 6. The CausalSmith banked eid_lp_svar_nonequiv attempts failed because the LP/SVAR restriction sets were unequal, sign exposure was normalized into tautology, and the cumulant margin was assumed rather than derived.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff:
{"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Pivot v1 states a flagship conjecture that a canonical extremal support-loss constant c_w(P) gives the LP/SVAR cumulant-label channel frontier, with a two-shock O(2) witness exhibit.","literature_map":"1. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) is the sanity anchor: LPs and VARs share population IRF estimands under unrestricted lag structures and common identifying restrictions.
2. Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner (2018, Econometrica) and Read (2022, Econometrics Journal) give the sign/zero rotation toolkit.
3. Gafarov, Meier, and Montiel Olea (2018, Journal of Econometrics) make support-function extrema over SVAR sets natural.
4. Wolf (2022, AEA P&P) names shock masquerading under weak sign restrictions.
5. Read (2022, RBA RDP) shows unit-effect normalization can make response sets unbounded, motivating non-normalized exposure.
6. Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne (2017, Journal of Econometrics) and Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen (2017, Journal of Econometrics) establish non-Gaussian SVAR labeling.
7. Lanne and Luoto (2021, JBES) warn that statistical identification rarely supplies economic interpretation.
8. Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, and Qian (2022, AEA P&P) and Hoesch, Lee, and Mesters (2024, QE) emphasize weak higher-moment regimes.
9. Drautzburg and Wright (2023, Journal of Econometrics) refine sign restrictions with independence, producing informative but sometimes nonconvex sets.

banked_reason: Stopped at D-0.5 after two hard rejects under flagship target: angle0 repeated already-known common-restriction equality and made c_w(P) circular/undefined at zero-channel boundary; angle1 had an internally contradicted c_w(P) definition because the infimum is zero at LP support maximizers, and its two-shock witness was a PMW no-gap corner rather than a positive separation witness.
